content.claim: The scale of the 6- and 8-year gimnázium phenomenon is measured inconsistently: enrollment in kisgimnázium tracks is reported at 9–10% of all students, while OECD 2019 data reportedly show 85% of Hungarian 15-year-olds attend a school that selects on performance (versus 43% OECD average), but these figures measure different phenomena and are not directly comparable.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The 85% figure is cited in an opinion piece and conflates overall school selectivity with 6/8-year tracks specifically
No direct access to the OECD 2019 source document provided in notes
The two metrics measure fundamentally different policy-relevant phenomena (overall selectivity vs. early-entry tracks) and should not be used interchangeably to assess 'problem size'
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
